titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.30

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=682 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:51:49.690324+00 | translation moa=683 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:51:51.62806+00

p.cl:
Dicit et in iure proponit coram vobis domine abbas de balmurinath’ unice iudex a sede apostolica delegate procurator prioris et conuentus cathedralis
ecclesie sancti andree contra magistrum priorissam . et moniales de Hadigton . quod cum ipsi prior et conuentus sint veri rectores ecclesie de Hadigton ̷ magister moni
alium de Hadigton priorissa et moniales predicte . quasdam decimas infra dictam parochiam de Hadigton . ad ipsos priorem et conuentum de Iure com
muni spectantes . videlicet . decimam vnius carucate terre in territorio de Helfstanford’ . que quondam spectabat ad territoriam de drem . et decimas
duarum bouatarum terre contentarum in veteri gardine de steueniston . et decimas illius terre que iacet inter vetus gardinum et villam de Hadigton . et
decimas dimidie acre terre iuxta molendinum de steueniston . et decimas croftorum quorundam burgensium de Hadigton . et minutas decimas de byris
et cottariorum de byris et de bernis . per aliqua tempora occupauerunt et in usus suos conuerterunt in preiudicium et dampnum non modicum ipsorum
prioris et conuentus necnon lesionem monasterii sui enormem . et adhuc ocupare non formidant quare petit procurator predictorum prioris et conuentus
predictas decimas ad ius et proprietatem predicte ecclesie sue de Hadigton . et sic ad monasterium sancti Andree per uos pertinere declarari . non obstantibus
literis instrumentis iuramentis penis renunciacionibus et confirmacionibus in communi forma a sede apostolica impetratis . per quemcumque seu
quoscumque priorem seu priores supradicti monasterium sancti Andree forsitas interpositis . ac predictos magistrum prioressam et moniales ab ocupacione dictarum
decimarum in futurum per uos compelli desistere sentencialiter et diffinitiue . petit etiam dictos magistrum priorissam et moniales in decimis predictis me
dio tempore perceptis quas estimant centum libras sterlingorum sibi condempnari et condempnatos ad solucionem compelli . petit etiam expensas circa in
gressum litis factas quas estimat decem libras sterlingorum et protestatur de faciendis in processu pro loco et tempore petendis vestrum implorans
officium in omnibus in quibus fuerit de Iure implorandum . non artans se ad omnia et singula prescise probanda . set eatenus optineat in premissis
quatenus probauerit .

The agent of the prior and community of St Andrews cathedral church says and argues in law before you, Lord Abbot of Balmerino, the only judge assigned by the Apostolic See, against the master, prioress, and the nuns of Haddington, that although they themselves – the prior and community – are the true rectors of the church of Haddington, the master of the nuns of Haddington, the prioress, and nuns aforesaid, have appropriated for some time certain teinds within the said parish of Haddington belonging to the prior and community by common right, namely, the teind of one ploughgate of land in the territory of Athelstaneford, which once belonged to the territory of Drem, and the teinds of two oxgangs of land contained in the old garden of Stevenson, and the teinds of the land that lies between the old garden and the toun of Haddington, and the teinds of half an acre of land between the mill of Stevenson and the teinds of the crofts of certain burgessses of Haddington, and the small teinds of Byres, and of the cottaries of Byres and of Barney, and they have turned them over to their own use to the no-small detriment and damage of the prior and community as well as the grave injury of their monastery, and until now have not been afraid to appropriate [them]; wherefore the agent of the aforesaid prior and convent seeks the aforesaid teinds as the right and property of his aforesaid church of Haddingotn, and thus by you to be declared to belong to the monastery of St Andrews, not withstanding the letters, instruments, judgments, penalties, renunciations, and confirmations obtained from the Apostolic See in the common form, interposed perchance through whichsoever prior or priors of the monastery of St Andrews; and the aforesaid master, prioress, and nuns be compelled by you to cease from the appropriation of the said teinds in future by definitive judicial sentence; he also seeks the said master, prioress, and nuns to be condemned in the aforesaid teinds received in the meantime which they assess as a hundred pounds sterling, and the condemned to be compelled to pay; and he also seeks costs incurred for entry of the lawsuit, which he assesses as ten pounds sterling; and he bears witness in respect of the things to be done in the legal proceedings for the place and time of asking, invoking your service in all things in which it was invoked by law, not constraining yourself to a categorical proving of all things in general and particular, but only so far as he may obtain so much as he may have proved in the foregoing.
